title: Live Blog! Morocco vs Norway Friendly Ahead of the 2026 World Cup
original_text:
Ødegaard equalizes and makes it 1-1.
Half time arrives with Morocco leading 1-0, but Noussair Mazraoui was forced off with an injury
Brahim gives Morocco an early lead, making it 1-0
The match is underway
The lineups

Morocco and Norway are putting the finishing touches on their preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a friendly match that will serve as a final test for two national teams arriving at the tournament with high expectations. The clash in New Jersey promises to be one of the most intriguing encounters of the final international window before the World Cup begins.

The countdown to the 2026 FIFA World Cup is entering its final stage, and national teams continue to make the most of every available opportunity to reach the tournament in peak condition. This Sunday, Morocco and Norway will face off in one of the most attractive international friendlies of the weekend, a match that will allow both sides to evaluate their readiness before the biggest event in world football.

The game will take place at Sports Illustrated Stadium in Harrison, New Jersey, a venue set to host two ambitious teams eager to finish their preparations on a positive note.

Both Morocco and Norway enter this match looking to fine-tune tactical details and assess player fitness ahead of their World Cup debuts. Coaching staffs will use the occasion to evaluate performances under competitive conditions and identify any final adjustments that may be needed.

For the Moroccan national team, this fixture represents the final rehearsal before a highly anticipated opening match against Brazil on June 13. The African side hopes to maintain the momentum it has built in recent years and prove once again that it can compete with the world's elite nations.

Meanwhile, Norway will be looking to build confidence before opening its World Cup campaign against Iraq on June 16. The European squad believes its current generation has the quality required to challenge any opponent during the tournament.

Although it is only a friendly, the match offers an ideal setting to evaluate strengths and address weaknesses before official competition begins. Both teams feature talented rosters and will be aiming to establish their best possible rhythm before the World Cup kicks off.

The encounter will also provide an opportunity to assess the physical condition of several key players expected to play major roles during the tournament, while allowing coaches to experiment with tactical variations that could become important in the weeks ahead.

With World Cup excitement building around the globe, Morocco and Norway know that this is one of their final opportunities to sharpen their preparations. What happens in New Jersey could provide important clues about the ambitions and potential of both teams heading into the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
